content.problemStatement:
Given two matrices of same size **M x N**, add them and print the resultant matrix.



For eg., here we can see, the sum of matrices `A` and `B` is the matrix `A+B` at the rightmost side.

Let's say we have two matrices A and B of the same dimensions (**M x N**).
The sum of these two matrices, denoted as C = A + B,
is another matrix of
the same dimensions where each element C[i][j] is the sum of the corresponding
elements A[i][j] and B[i][j].


Input Format

The first line of input will contain two space separated integers NNN and MMM, denoting the number of rows and columns of the two input matrices.
Next NNN lines contains MMM space separated integers, the elements of first matrix.
Similarly, next NNN lines contains MMM space separated integers, the elements of second matrix.


Output Format
Output NNN lines contains MMM space separated integers, the elements of resultant matrix.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers N and M — the number of rows and columns of the matrices. The next N lines each contain M space-separated integers describing matrix A. The following N lines each contain M space-separated integers describing matrix B.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output N lines, each line containing M space-separated integers – the elements of the resultant matrix C = A + B. Print a newline after each row. Test cases are output sequentially without extra blank lines.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each element of the first matrix is added to the corresponding element of the second matrix, producing the shown 2×3 result.

hidden.label: tricky edge (large values with small offsets)

solution.approach: For each test case we read the dimensions N and M. We store the first matrix in a 2‑D array (or vector). While reading the second matrix we directly output the sum of the corresponding element from the first matrix and the current element. This avoids storing both matrices simultaneously and runs in linear time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * M) – each element is visited exactly once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N * M) for storing the first matrix (the second matrix is processed on the fly).
